faq.question: Why am I seeing `ExperimentalWarning: buffer.Blob is an experimental feature` in my logs?
faq.answer: This warning is generated by Node.js when the `undici` library, which the plugin uses for network communication, accesses certain experimental features. This transition occurred when the plugin replaced deprecated libraries (`request` and `request-promise-native`) to ensure long-term compatibility. This message is safe to ignore and has no impact on the functionality or stability of the plugin.
faq.semantic_abstract: Migration from deprecated HTTP request libraries to `undici` triggers a Node.js `ExperimentalWarning` regarding the `buffer.Blob` feature. This warning is a side-effect of internal dependency updates and does not affect plugin operation or reliability.
faq.alternative_resolution: Explicitly document this specific warning in a 'Troubleshooting' or 'Known Issues' section of the README to reduce user concern, as it is a common point of confusion for those monitoring logs.
